author: Coffee Nodes
msg: 40 per sim, no? so just buy a sim under 40, and then add a new sim later?

author: LIIICHT
msg: No. Youll be reimbursed for a TOTAL $40. Its not an ongoing reimbursement.

author: LIIICHT
msg:
"Thank you for reaching out with your question. The reimbursement amount of up to $40.00 mentioned for your prepaid SIM purchase is a total maximum limit, not a monthly amount. This means the maximum reimbursement you can receive for the plan or SIM purchase is $40.00 in total. If you need further clarification or assistance with your account or plans, please feel free to contact us anytime.
Best regards,
Scout & Runner Support Team"

author: Coffee Nodes
msg:
"There is a maximum of $40 USD for any one SIM refund. "

"There is a maximum amount of $40 per refund. You cannot top up a SIM in the Scout Role. If use up all the balance / time, the recommendation is to purchase another SIM and register it."

"No limit on number of SIMs you can buy and submit a refund request for. Only limit is $40 USD for any one refund request"

author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK

author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK
msg:
Yep, it's still works! 

V1 which is on the mainnet is very basic. But live and working

author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK
msg:
V2 which is linked above is on the testnet but interacts with all NFT's (unity included) minted on the WMC. Plus a whole lot of backend stuff which should improve UX.

In all honesty, everything now works as intended. It's just a matter of transferring it onto the mainnet.

Then everyone can list their unity nodes 😂. 

Only limitation being, WMC does not have a stablecoin yet. So all transactions are in wmtx.

author: Flavius
msg: Awesome, stablecoin or not at least there is a way. I really hope the project picks up by summer in case I need to let go of my node by then, but best case scenario that won't be needed 😅

author: Abdus
msg: Are there additional costs like paying 1.99? For the moment they are not making ULOs pay that fee granted, but about in the future? Also it felt at some point they mentioned different tasks would have buy-in fees. Don’t quote me on this, but I have the impression it was either said or implied. author: Willian | Nuvola
msg:
I wouldn't necessarily see us as the highway builders but the "Uber franchise owners". ULOs are the drivers of the cars you are providing (licenses) that will be driven on the Unity highway. 

There are two choices for the tolls, the Uber drivers you hired can pay them or you as the franchise owner. 🫡

At least that's how I see it. 

Unity are the ones bulding the highway and bringing on the clients that will be riding on those Ubers.
